diagrams.title: Basic Tree Structure
diagrams.description: A general tree diagram showing nodes connected by edges, illustrating fundamental tree concepts including root (if applicable), parent nodes, child nodes, leaf nodes, and internal nodes. This provides the foundation for understanding rooted trees and path lengths.
diagrams.purpose: Introduce the basic terminology and structure of trees in discrete mathematics, establishing visual familiarity with nodes, edges, and relationships before introducing path length concepts.

diagrams.title: Rooted Tree with Level Numbers
diagrams.description: A rooted tree diagram with a designated root node at the top, showing the level/depth of each node. The root is at level 0, its children at level 1, grandchildren at level 2, etc. Each level is visually separated and labeled with level numbers.
diagrams.purpose: Explain the concept of a rooted tree and how depth/level is measured from the root, which is essential for understanding path lengths in subsequent diagrams.

diagrams.title: Path Lengths and Tree Height
diagrams.description: A rooted tree showing path lengths from the root to each node (number of edges traversed). The longest path from root to any leaf is highlighted to demonstrate tree height. Each node displays its path length from the root.
elements: path length labels at each node
leaf nodes with maximum depth indicated
diagrams.purpose: Visualize and compute path lengths in rooted trees, introducing the concept of tree height as the maximum depth, reinforcing the relationship between root, levels, and path lengths.
